paintings.title: Composition
paintings.location: Pushkin State Museum of Fine Arts
paintings.story: Miró painted this Composition in 1927, a few years after joining the Surrealist group in Paris in 1924. The sparse canvases of this period, eventually dubbed his dream paintings, fit well within the dream-like automatism espoused by the group and shed the cluttered, chaotic focus of his earlier work. Through the mid-1920s Miró developed a pictorial sign language that would remain central throughout the rest of his career, and in works of these years the remaining figures grew ever fewer and more simplified.
funFacts: André Breton described Miró as "the most surrealist of us all".
Miró said that at night in his Rue Blomet studio in Paris, sometimes having had no supper, he saw shapes on the ceiling and jotted them down in a notebook.
In 1926, the year before this painting, Miró collaborated with Max Ernst on designs for ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev.
